purpose: Direction-level PowerLit evidence-strength baselines for recurring research under D:\Research numeric project folders.
source_rule: Treat top-level D:\Research directories whose names start with a digit as recurring research directions; group them into broad evidence bars rather than project-specific bars.

directions.label: 概率潮流
directions.scope: Analytical probabilistic power flow, AQPPF, Bayesian/AQGMM updates, multi-period and three-phase uncertainty propagation, and voltage distribution reconstruction.

queries.query: probabilistic power flow uncertainty distribution network renewable Gaussian mixture voltage distribution
three phase probabilistic load flow Bayesian update stochastic load flow distribution system

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Main text gives the uncertainty source, sample or moment construction, grid object, voltage or branch-flow target, and aggregation denominator.
At least two network scales or a scale-plus-variant design are visible; three-phase or multi-period claims need matching systems, not only single-phase proxies.
Comparison includes Monte Carlo or high-fidelity numerical reference plus at least one analytical or data-driven probabilistic baseline.
Metrics must cover both moment-level error and distribution or risk-level error when distributional claims are made.
Sensitivity or ablation is required for mixture number, sample size, covariance structure, linearization order, or update sample count when these drive the claim.
Solver, tolerance, random seed or initialization, and fallback/switch rules are manuscript-facing when optimization or clustering affects results.
extra_gate_for_inverse_or_diagnostic_cla: The paper must state a non-substitutable use case where the inverse reconstruction is needed rather than a stronger forward probabilistic power-flow pipeline.
If a forward baseline wins on accuracy or runtime, diagnostic value must be evidenced by identifiability, feasibility, boundary, observability, or missing-measurement results.
A main-case module that is disabled or replaced by a baseline must be labelled as such in the table, figure caption, and discussion.
blockers: Only reporting case-analysis numbers without derivation, algorithm, baseline protocol, and metric definitions.
Claiming general superiority when a relevant forward PLF/GMM baseline wins on the primary accuracy and runtime metrics.
Leaving extreme diagnostic quantities or local CSV paths outside the manuscript-facing reproducibility record.

directions.label: 风险、韧性与成本分摊
directions.scope: Extreme-weather risk, typhoon resilience, risk screening, long-run incremental cost pricing, tail dependence, and risk attribution.

queries.query: power system resilience typhoon extreme weather risk screening contingency outage source load uncertainty
long run incremental cost risk allocation distribution network tail dependence reliability pricing

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Hazard, grid state, load/renewable uncertainty, contingency definition, and resilience or cost object are all visible in the manuscript.
The paper separates screening or attribution claims from calibrated prediction claims unless event-level forecast baselines and calibration metrics are included.
Case study includes multi-event or multi-scenario stress evidence, not only one selected storm or one selected tail case.
Baselines include deterministic stress, independence or Gaussian dependence, and at least one method-family competitor when claiming modelling value.
Metrics must include ranking, risk magnitude, violation or outage consequence, and cost/allocation consistency where relevant.
Sensitivity is required for tail threshold, dependence strength, scenario truncation, restoration or topology assumptions, and screening budget.
blockers: Using risk-screening results as if they were calibrated event forecasts.
No replay or high-fidelity check for the ranked high-risk states.
No explanation of what practical decision changes because of the risk or cost allocation.

directions.label: 概率优化运行与安全约束调度
directions.scope: Chance-constrained OPF, probabilistic OPF, SCUC/TSCOPF with uncertainty, topology or projection based operation, and risk-layer optimization.

queries.query: chance constrained optimal power flow probabilistic OPF uncertainty AC power flow risk constraint
security constrained unit commitment transient stability constrained optimal power flow uncertainty renewable

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Objective, constraints, uncertainty model, violation probability or risk layer, and controllable variables are visible before the case study.
The optimization benchmark must use the same information set, grid case, risk budget, and feasibility check as the proposed method.
Primary metrics include operating cost, violation or infeasibility probability, conservatism, runtime, and AC or dynamic replay validity when claimed.
At least one scalability dimension is required: larger network, more scenarios, more time periods, or more contingencies.
Sensitivity is required for risk budget, sample size or ambiguity set, solver tolerance, and key approximation order.
If an approximation is used, the paper must show where feasibility is checked and what happens when the approximation fails.
blockers: Optimization model is stated but solver, tolerance, and feasibility recovery are absent.
Only comparing against deterministic OPF while claiming probabilistic superiority.
No runtime or scalability evidence for a method advertised as practical.

directions.label: 配网拓扑与参数辨识
directions.scope: Distribution topology identification, line/device support recovery, admittance/Ybus parameter estimation, AMI or limited-measurement inference, and three-phase local joint recovery.
claim_classes: distribution system state or model inference

queries.query: distribution network topology identification parameter estimation smart meter AMI voltage measurement
distribution system state estimation topology detection admittance matrix Ybus three phase

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Measurement setting is explicit: available channels, sampling rate, noise, phase coverage, synchronization, missing data, and observability limits.
Topology and parameter claims use separate metrics; support recovery, line identification, impedance/admittance error, and voltage replay should not be conflated.
Baselines include at least one topology or parameter identification method with comparable measurement access.
Case evidence includes standard feeders plus stress variations in noise, sample size, loading diversity, switch state, or hidden device assumptions.
Boundary cases are mandatory when recovery is local, support-limited, radial-only, or assumes known candidate neighborhoods.
Reproducibility includes data-generation rules, OpenDSS or feeder source, random seed, thresholding rules, and exact evaluation denominators.
blockers: A perfect F1 table without noise, sample-size, or topology-change stress.
Claiming arbitrary full-graph recovery when the algorithm is local or candidate-neighborhood limited.
No separation between measurement infeasibility, optimization failure, and physical non-identifiability.

directions.label: 矩方法、SDP松弛与可行性证书
directions.scope: Moment mappings, semidefinite relaxation, polynomial power-flow models, feasibility certificates, identifiability diagnostics, and boundary characterization across PLF and OPF papers.

queries.query: moment relaxation semidefinite programming power flow feasibility certificate polynomial optimization
power system identifiability observability certificate semidefinite relaxation voltage feasibility

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: The paper explains why the theorem, PSD constraint, rank condition, certificate, or diagnostic quantity is needed before proving or using it.
Mathematical claims include assumptions, dimensions, regularity or Slater-type conditions when certificates depend on SDP duality.
Numerical evidence reports certificate values, residuals, eigenvalue or rank diagnostics, solver tolerance, and weak/inconclusive handling.
A boundary or transition case is required when the claim is identifiability, feasibility, or relaxation tightness.
If the method is slower or less accurate than a baseline, the evidence must show what the certificate or diagnostic changes in decision making.
The manuscript must distinguish a necessary structure for identifiability from one chosen parameterization or engineering prior.
blockers: Dropping a PSD or certificate section into the paper without explaining its role in the method chain.
Calling one structured parameterization necessary when the evidence only supports low-dimensional regularization in general.
Reporting infeasibility or a dual certificate without stating regularity assumptions, solver tolerance, or weak-infeasibility handling.

directions.label: 电网规划
directions.scope: Transmission and distribution planning, expansion, hosting-capacity planning, resilience-oriented investment, reliability planning, storage/DER siting and sizing, and uncertainty-aware long-horizon grid decisions.

queries.query: power system planning transmission distribution expansion resilience renewable uncertainty investment reliability
distribution network planning hosting capacity DER storage siting sizing reliability uncertainty

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Planning object, horizon, asset candidates, investment variables, operating constraints, and uncertainty scenarios are defined before results.
The case study must connect investment decisions to operational feasibility, reliability, resilience, hosting capacity, emissions, or cost outcomes.
Baselines include deterministic planning, existing planning rule or heuristic, and a close stochastic/robust/resilience planning competitor when claiming methodological value.
Evidence includes at least one scale or scenario variation: renewable penetration, load growth, contingency set, hazard severity, budget, or asset cost.
Metrics must report investment cost and operational or reliability consequence together; cost-only or feasibility-only evidence is insufficient for planning claims.
The paper states data sources, representative-day or scenario construction, solver settings, and any simplification that affects long-horizon decisions.
blockers: Treating a single operating case as planning evidence without an investment horizon or asset-decision layer.
Claiming planning optimality without reporting feasibility under out-of-sample or stressed operating states.
No comparison against deterministic, robust, stochastic, or rule-based planning baselines with comparable candidate assets.

directions.label: 电网调度运行
directions.scope: Economic dispatch, unit commitment, OPF, voltage/reactive-power operation, security-constrained dispatch, real-time operation, DER coordination, and emergency or resilience-oriented operational decisions.

queries.query: power system dispatch operation economic dispatch unit commitment optimal power flow renewable uncertainty
distribution system operation voltage control reactive power demand response DER real time dispatch

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Dispatch interval, controllable resources, network constraints, uncertainty information timing, and feasibility recovery are explicit.
Baselines include deterministic dispatch and a close stochastic, robust, chance-constrained, or model-predictive operational method when relevant.
Primary metrics include operating cost, security or violation probability, voltage/line/reserve feasibility, curtailment or load shedding, and runtime.
Case evidence must include multi-period or multi-scenario operation when the method claims real operation rather than static set-point computation.
Sensitivity is required for forecast error, risk budget, reserve margin, contingency set, communication/measurement availability, or dispatch horizon.
The manuscript distinguishes offline calculation, day-ahead schedule, real-time recourse, and emergency control; evidence must match the claimed layer.
blockers: Claiming real-time operation while only reporting offline optimization results without runtime and recourse evidence.
No AC or high-fidelity feasibility replay when the method uses a simplified dispatch model.
Mixing day-ahead, intraday, and real-time claims without stating information timing and control authority.

directions.label: 机组组合
directions.scope: Deterministic, stochastic, robust, security-constrained, and renewable-aware unit commitment, including reserve co-optimization, network-constrained commitment, and uncertainty-aware day-ahead scheduling.

queries.query: security constrained unit commitment renewable uncertainty reserve co-optimization stochastic robust
unit commitment network constraints ramping storage demand response wind power uncertainty

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Commitment horizon, time resolution, unit parameters, minimum up/down time, ramping, startup/shutdown cost, reserve requirement, and network or security constraints are visible.
Uncertainty-aware UC claims must state scenario, ambiguity, reserve, or chance-constraint construction and the information available at day-ahead and recourse stages.
Baselines include deterministic UC and a close stochastic, robust, chance-constrained, or reserve-rule UC method with comparable network and reserve settings.
Metrics include total operating cost, startup/shutdown cost, reserve cost, curtailment or load shedding, violation or infeasibility rate, runtime, and MIP gap.
Evidence includes system scale, number of units, time periods, scenarios or contingencies, and at least one sensitivity to uncertainty, reserve, or renewable penetration.
The paper reports solver, gap, time limit, decomposition settings, and any relaxation or heuristic recovery used to obtain feasible schedules.
blockers: Calling an OPF or economic dispatch result unit commitment without binary commitment states and inter-temporal unit constraints.
No deterministic or stochastic/robust UC baseline under the same reserve and network assumptions.
No feasibility replay or reserve adequacy check for schedules claimed to handle uncertainty.

directions.label: 优化调度
directions.scope: Optimal dispatch, economic dispatch, multi-period dispatch, active distribution network dispatch, DER/storage/demand-response coordination, and emergency or resilience-oriented redispatch.

queries.query: economic dispatch optimal dispatch renewable uncertainty multi period power system operation
active distribution network optimal dispatch energy storage demand response DER voltage constraints

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Dispatch layer, time scale, controllable devices, objective, network constraints, uncertainty timing, and recourse or feasibility recovery are stated.
Baselines include deterministic dispatch and the closest stochastic, robust, MPC, OPF, or heuristic dispatch method applicable to the same resources.
Metrics include operating cost, voltage/line/reserve feasibility, curtailment, load shedding, loss, emissions if claimed, runtime, and convergence or optimality gap.
Multi-period claims need horizon-level evidence, not a single-period set point; real-time claims need runtime and update or recourse evidence.
Sensitivity covers forecast error, storage capacity, DER penetration, reserve margin, dispatch interval, or communication/measurement availability.
Simplified model results must be checked through AC power flow, OpenDSS, dynamic replay, or another high-fidelity feasibility layer when operational feasibility is claimed.
blockers: Claiming optimized operation without defining the dispatch layer and information timing.
No operational baseline under comparable resource and network constraints.
No high-fidelity feasibility check when a linearized or surrogate dispatch model is used.

directions.label: 概率最优潮流
directions.scope: Probabilistic OPF, chance-constrained OPF, stochastic OPF, risk-constrained OPF, distributionally robust OPF, and exact or approximate violation-probability control in AC/DC power-flow optimization.

queries.query: chance constrained optimal power flow probabilistic OPF renewable uncertainty violation probability
stochastic optimal power flow risk constrained distributionally robust OPF AC power flow uncertainty

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Uncertainty model, OPF objective, controllable variables, AC/DC network model, risk or violation-probability definition, and risk budget are visible.
The paper states whether constraints are individual, joint, empirical, analytical, robust, or distributionally robust and how probabilities are evaluated.
Baselines include deterministic OPF and at least one SAA, chance-constrained, robust, or stochastic OPF competitor with the same uncertainty access.
Metrics include expected or scheduled cost, empirical violation probability, risk-budget satisfaction, conservatism, voltage/line constraint violations, and runtime.
Validation includes Monte Carlo or out-of-sample replay against the final dispatch/control policy, not only the optimization objective value.
Sensitivity covers risk budget, sample size, distributional assumptions, uncertainty penetration, linearization or relaxation order, and solver tolerance.
blockers: Calling an OPF probabilistic when uncertainty only appears in background text and not in constraints, objective, or evaluation.
No out-of-sample or Monte Carlo violation-probability verification.
Comparing only with deterministic OPF while claiming superiority over probabilistic OPF methods.

directions.label: 配电网三相再平衡
directions.scope: Three-phase distribution feeder rebalancing, phase swapping or phase switching, load/DER phase allocation, unbalance mitigation, voltage balancing, neutral-current reduction, and three-phase operational reconfiguration.

queries.query: three phase distribution network phase balancing phase switching reconfiguration unbalance voltage
load phase allocation DER phase connection voltage unbalance neutral current distribution feeder

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: The three-phase feeder model, phase connectivity, load/DER phase data, controllable phase-switching or allocation variables, and operational constraints are explicit.
Metrics include voltage unbalance factor or phase voltage deviation, neutral current, phase loading imbalance, losses, voltage violations, switching actions, and customer or device constraints.
Baselines include no-rebalancing operation, heuristic phase balancing, topology or phase-switching baseline, and a close optimization or control method when claiming methodological value.
Case evidence uses unbalanced three-phase feeders and stress variations in load asymmetry, PV/DER penetration, missing phase data, switch budget, or measurement noise.
The paper reports whether phase actions are planning actions, offline reconfiguration, or real-time switching; switching frequency and implementation constraints must match that layer.
High-fidelity three-phase power-flow validation, OpenDSS or equivalent feeder replay, and exact denominator for phase-level metrics are manuscript-facing.
blockers: Using a balanced single-phase model while claiming three-phase rebalancing.
No metric for voltage or current unbalance despite claiming phase balancing.
Ignoring switching cost, customer interruption, device limits, or feasibility of phase changes when recommending rebalancing actions.

directions.label: 定价方法
directions.scope: Transmission/distribution pricing, locational marginal pricing, long-run incremental cost, network tariff, risk-aware pricing, cost allocation, congestion pricing, and reliability or resilience price signals.

queries.query: locational marginal pricing distribution network pricing long run incremental cost cost allocation congestion
network tariff pricing reliability risk allocation resilience distribution system electricity price signal

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Pricing object, payer/payee boundary, cost or marginal signal definition, settlement interval, and network or reliability constraint source are explicit.
The paper proves or numerically verifies key economic properties claimed: cost recovery, revenue adequacy, incentive compatibility, fairness, efficiency, volatility, or risk attribution.
Baselines include existing tariff, LMP/DLMP, LRIC, postage-stamp, marginal-cost, or allocation methods appropriate to the claimed pricing layer.
Case evidence reports both price/allocation outcomes and the grid-side consequence that justifies the price signal.
Sensitivity is required for load growth, renewable uncertainty, congestion, reliability target, risk aversion, or asset-cost assumptions.
The manuscript separates engineering pricing, regulatory tariff design, and market-clearing price claims; evidence must match the institutional layer.
blockers: Presenting a numerical index as a price without settlement object, cost-recovery logic, or payer/payee definition.
No comparison to existing pricing or allocation method used in the same network layer.
Claiming economic efficiency or fairness without defining and testing the economic metric.

directions.label: 电力市场设计
directions.scope: Market clearing, bidding, reserve and ancillary-service design, flexibility markets, distribution-level markets, DER aggregation, demand response, transactive energy, and uncertainty-aware market mechanisms.

queries.query: electricity market design market clearing reserve ancillary service renewable uncertainty stochastic market
distribution electricity market flexibility market transactive energy peer to peer trading demand response DER aggregation

minimum_for_full_paper_8_9: Market product, participants, bidding variables, clearing objective, settlement rule, network constraints, and uncertainty timing are defined.
If incentive, strategic, fairness, or market-power claims are made, the paper must include the corresponding game-theoretic, equilibrium, or counterfactual evidence.
Baselines include current market rule, deterministic clearing, social-welfare clearing, or a close stochastic/robust/flexibility-market mechanism.
Metrics include social welfare or total cost, participant surplus or payments, price volatility, uplift or revenue adequacy, reliability/security outcome, and computational tractability.
Case evidence should include stress on renewable penetration, congestion, reserve scarcity, participant behavior, demand response, or DER aggregation scale.
The manuscript distinguishes market design from operational dispatch and from pricing formula design; the evidence must show the institutional mechanism, not only an OPF price.
blockers: Calling an optimization model a market design without participant, bid, clearing, and settlement definitions.
No strategic or incentive evidence while claiming truthful, fair, or efficient market behavior.
No comparison with existing clearing or settlement rules under the same network and uncertainty setting.
